items.headline: Special Weather Statement issued April 21 at 9:29PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
A Bering Sea Low will bring a front into the Western Interior
with snow and wind beginning Wednesday morning/midday from Holy
Cross to McGrath. The front will move northward through the day
and will be in Ambler around midnight Thursday morning. As the front
lifts northward, temperatures on the south side of it will
approach and exceed 32 degrees. This leaves a chance for rain to
mix in, especially south of Galena Wednesday evening/night.
Snowfall totals through Thursday morning from Anvik to Mc
items.areas: Upper Kobuk Valleys; Lower Koyukuk Valley; Middle Yukon Valley; Lower Yukon and Innoko Valleys; North Slopes of the Western Alaska Range; Upper Kuskokwim Valley

items.headline: Special Weather Statement issued April 21 at 5:50PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
An arctic front past through the area early today and brought snow
showers and breezy conditions. As the winds calm this evening,
temperatures will begin to drop rapidly. Fortunately, the wind
helped dry out many wet spots. However, any remaining wet spots
will likely freeze resulting in patchy ice. Please use caution
tonight and especially tomorrow morning as there may be slick
spots around the Interior.
items.areas: Yukon Flats; White Mountains and High Terrain South of the Yukon River; Fortymile Country; Upper Tanana Valley; Delta Junction; Upper Chena River Valley; Tanana Flats; Eielson AFB and Salcha; Goldstream Valley and Nenana Hills; Chatanika River Valley; Two Rivers; Fairbanks Metro Area; Nenana; Northern Denali Borough; Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp

items.headline: Wind Advisory issued April 21 at 5:20PM AKDT until April 24 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...South winds 35 to 45 mph with gusts up to 65 mph expected.

* WHERE...Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp.

* WHEN...From 10 PM Wednesday to 4 PM AKDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.
items.areas: Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp
items.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

items.headline: Blizzard Warning issued April 21 at 5:11PM AKDT until April 23 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Blizzard conditions expected. Total snow accumulations
between 4 and 8 inches. Winds gusting as high as 55 mph.

* WHERE...Lower Yukon River and Yukon Delta Coast.

* WHEN...From 10 PM this evening to 4 PM AKDT Thursday.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be very difficult. Areas of blowing snow
could significantly reduce visibility.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Strongest winds will occur Tuesday night into
Wednesday morning, then weaken gradually through the afternoon.
Another pulse of wind will mov
items.areas: Yukon Delta Coast; Lower Yukon River
items.instruction:
Travel should be restricted to emergencies only. If you must travel,
have a winter survival kit with you. If you get stranded, stay with
your vehicle.

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 21 at 2:50PM AKDT until April 22 at 10:00AM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow and blowing snow expected. Total snow accumulations up
to 5 inches. Winds gusting as high as 40 mph. Visibility reduced
to 1/2 mile or less at times. Ice accumulations of a light glaze.

* WHERE...Nunivak Island and the Kuskokwim Delta.

* WHEN...Until 10 AM AKDT Wednesday.

* IMPACTS...Visibilities reduced to one half mile or less due to
blowing snow. Travel could be very difficult. Areas of blowing
snow could significantly reduce visibility. Gusty winds could blow
around unsecure
items.areas: Kuskokwim Delta Coast and Nunivak Island; Interior Kuskokwim Delta
items.instruction: Slow down and use caution while traveling.
